title: Rich Gooey Slovakian Pork Belly Stir-fry
description: A rich and gooey pork belly dish thats perfect for a sick-day treat, made in the comfort of your own kitchen with no blanching required.

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.amount: 2 tbsp fresh or 1 tbsp dried lemon zest

instructions: Pat the pork belly dry with paper towels and sprinkle a pinch of salt evenly over both sides.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the pork belly skin-side down and cook for about 5-7 minutes until nicely browned on one side.
Flip the pork belly to the other side and cook another 3-4 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from heat and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tbsp oil. Add sliced onions and minced garlic. Cook for about 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add red pepper flakes (if using) and stir-fry for another minute. Mix in Dijon mustard, lemon juice, and sesame seeds. Stir well to combine.

Return the pork belly to the skillet and spoon the sauce evenly over it. Cover with a lid or aluminum foil and place in the preheated oven. Bake for 15-20 minutes, until the internal temperature of the pork reaches at least 145°F (63°C).
Remove from oven and let rest for about 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
Serve hot as a snack or side dish with steamed vegetables.
